latest.title: Notice of Results and Investor Presentation
latest.content:
12 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 8579D
IG Design Group PLC
12 May 2026
12 May 2026
IG Design Group Plc
('Design Group' or the 'Company')
Notice of Results and Investor Presentation
Following the release of the Company's Full Year Results on Tuesday 16 June 2026, the management of IG Design Group Plc, a leading designer, innovator and manufacturer across various celebration and creative categories, will be hosting a virtual presentation and Q&A for investors at 11am.
The presentation is open to all existing and potential investors and will be given by Stewart Gilliland, Interim Executive Chair and Rohan Cummings, Chief Financial Officer.
To register to attend, please use the following link:
https://us06web.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_vIVPLO2WTTKW2WBDVaGO1Q#/registration
Additionally, management will be hosting a virtual presentation and Q&A for analysts at 9am on 16 June 2026. To register to attend, please email
designgroup@almastrategic.com
For further information, please contact:
​
IG Design Group Plc
Gerald Kuehr, Chief Executive Officer Designate
Rohan Cummings, Chief Financial Officer
Tel: +44 (0)1525 887310
Canaccord Genuity Limited (Nomad and Broker)
Bobbie Hilliam
Elizabeth Halley-Stott
Tel: +44 (0)20 7523 8000
Alma Strategic Communications
Rebecca Sanders-Hewett
Sam Modlin
Will Merison
Tel: +44 (0)20 3405 0205
designgroup@al
mastrategic.com
Notes to Editors:
IG Design Group plc is one of the world's leading designers, innovators and manufacturers of gift packaging, greeting cards, stationery, creative play products and related items. The Group designs, sources and manufactures a broad portfolio of products across multiple categories and occasions, encompassing every day, seasonal and celebration ranges.
The Group operates across key international markets, with a strong presence in the United Kingdom, Europe and Australia. Its products are sold to a diverse customer base including major retailers and supermarkets such as Tesco, Costco and Aldi, as well as discounters, online platforms and independent stores. The Group's heritage brand, Tom Smith, holds a Royal Warrant for the supply of Christmas crackers and Christmas wrapping paper to the Royal Family, a distinction it has held continuously since 1906.
Across IG Design Group's global teams, it manages a portfolio of more than 22,000 SKUs, supplying over 550 million units annually to customers in approximately 70 countries. The Group operates a vertically integrated model, combining in‑house design expertise with global sourcing, manufacturing, distribution and fulfilment capabilities. Its manufacturing footprint includes three facilities located in Wales, the Netherlands and Poland. This integrated platform enables the Group to deliver innovative, responsibly sourced products at scale, while maintaining cost efficiency, speed to market and flexibility.
IG Design Group plc is listed on the AIM market of the London Stock Exchange.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.title: Trading Update and Acquisition of Glenart, S.A.
latest.content:
30 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4836C
IG Design Group PLC
30 April 2026
30 April 2026
IG Design Group plc
(the "Company", the "Group" or "Design Group")
FY2026 Trading Update and Acquisition of Glenart, S.A.
Revenue, profit and cash ahead of expectations
IG Design Group plc, a leading designer, innovator and manufacturer across various celebration and creative categories, provides an update on its financial performance for the year ended 31 March 2026 and post year-end material developments. The trading update refers to the continuing business of the Group only, excluding adjusting items and losses related to the disposal of DG Americas announced in May 2025.
Trading Update
Following the positive trading update issued in February, the Group has continued to experience good trading momentum, and the Board is pleased to announce that FY2026 results are expected to be ahead of market consensus and Board expectations*. The Group expects to report FY2026 revenues of c$292 million and adjusted operating profit of c$12.8 million, representing an adjusted operating margin of 4.4%, and delivering adjusted profit before tax of c$11.5 million**.
Cash has significantly exceeded expectations with year-end net cash of c$72 million. Excluding c$35 million of outflow in the year relating to DG Americas, the continuing Group generated over $20 million of cash during the year, reflecting strong working capital management and $4 million of proceeds from the sale of a surplus UK warehouse. The Group has a strong balance sheet position and an update on the future capital allocation policy will be provided in our full-year results.
The Board acknowledges the ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty, including cost pressures, inflation, and softer consumer confidence, and remains cautious in its outlook. However, the Board is encouraged by FY2026 results and the clear outperformance against expectations set earlier in the year. Together with the recently announced appointment of a Group CEO, this provides a solid platform for continued progress against the Group's long-term strategic growth pillars.
Acquisition of Glenart, S.A.
The Group is pleased to announce the acquisition of 100 percent of the equity interest in Glenart Trading Proprietary Limited, Marcorp Marketing Consultants Proprietary Limited and Glenart Trading Limited (collectively referred to as 'Glenart S.A.') for a total consideration of c£5.3 million (including c£1.3m of cash within the business and working capital adjustments). The consideration comprises an initial cash payment of c£3.4 million, with deferred consideration payable over three years, and additional potential earn-out consideration contingent on future business performance. The acquisition is financed from working capital and expected to be earnings enhancing. For the year ended 28 February 2026, the business reported unaudited revenues of c£4million, PBIT of c£0.8 million and EBITDA of c£1million, from a net asset base of c£2.7 million.
Glenart S.A. is a South African manufacturer within the Group's "Celebrate" category, with a particular strength in crackers, and is therefore highly complementary to the Group's existing operations. The business generates the majority of its revenues in South Africa, the UK and the USA, and operates across similar mass-market and trade channels to the Group. The Board considers the acquisition a bolt-on, strategically aligned and a compelling opportunity to expand the Group's geographical presence in a complementary product offering.
*
Current guidance: revenue $280-285 million, adjusted operating margin 4% and cash $55-60 million. We believe consensus to be revenue $282 million, adjusted operating margin 4% and cash $56 million.
**
The Group intends to adopt GBP as its presentational currency for consolidated financial statements, replacing USD, effective from the FY2026 full-year results.
For further information, please contact:
IG Design Group plc
Tel: +44 (0)1525 887310
Stewart Gilliland, Interim Executive Chair
Rohan Cummings, Chief Financial Officer
Canaccord Genuity Limited (Nomad and Broker)
Tel: +44 (0)20 7523 8000
Bobbie Hilliam
Elizabeth Halley-Stott
Alma Strategic Communications
Tel: +44 (0)20 3405 0205
Rebecca Sanders-Hewett
designgroup@almastrategic.com
Sam Modlin
Will Merison
Notes to Editors:
IG Design Group plc is one of the world's leading designers, innovators and manufacturers of gift packaging, greeting cards, stationery, creative play products and related items. The Group designs, sources and manufactures a broad portfolio of products across multiple categories and occasions, encompassing every day, seasonal and celebration ranges.
The Group operates across key international markets, with a strong presence in the United Kingdom, Europe and Australia. Its products are sold to a diverse customer base including major retailers and supermarkets such as Tesco, Costco and Aldi, as well as discounters, online platforms and independent stores. The Group's heritage brand, Tom Smith, holds a Royal Warrant for the supply of Christmas crackers and Christmas wrapping paper to the Royal Family, a distinction it has held continuously since 1906.
Across IG Design Group's global teams, it manages a portfolio of more than 22,000 SKUs, supplying over 550 million units annually to customers in approximately 70 countries. The Group operates a vertically integrated model, combining in‑house design expertise with global sourcing, manufacturing, distribution and fulfilment capabilities. Its manufacturing footprint includes three facilities located in Wales, the Netherlands and Poland. This integrated platform enables the Group to deliver innovative, responsibly sourced products at scale, while maintaining cost efficiency, speed to market and flexibility.
IG Design Group plc is listed on the AIM market of the London Stock Exchange.

company_30s.what_they_do: IGR Ltd runs a network of warehouses and distribution hubs across the UK, providing flexible storage and logistics services for e‑commerce and manufacturing clients.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits in the backbone of the UK supply chain. With e‑commerce growth, demand for last‑mile and cross‑border warehousing is rising, giving IGR a clear niche to capture volume and margin expansion.
company_30s.current_state: IGR is in a restructuring phase – it has sold a surplus UK warehouse, reduced its capital base, and appointed a new CEO. The focus is on trimming debt and streamlining operations, but cash burn remains a concern while the company seeks new contracts to stabilize revenue.

rns_narrative: Management and capital‑structure moves: a new CEO, director shareholdings, and a capital‑reduction plan signal an attempt to reset the balance sheet.
Operational consolidation: the sale of a UK warehouse and the acquisition of Glenart, S.A. show a shift toward a leaner, more profitable portfolio.
Financial reporting: the upcoming investor presentation on 16 June will deliver the latest earnings and cash‑flow numbers, which could confirm whether the restructuring is paying off.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism around a possible turnaround after the June results.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders point to director buying and the new CEO as a bullish sign, hoping the June report will reveal a turnaround.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others highlight the persistent cash burn and lack of revenue growth, warning that the company could slip further if earnings miss expectations.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Until the June presentation, the market remains largely silent. If the numbers are better than expected, sentiment could swing sharply to the upside.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no search chatter. This means a good news event could trigger a surprise rally.

near_term_catalysts: June 16 investor presentation – earnings, cash‑flow, and guidance.
Any sign of new contracts or partnership announcements, especially from the recently acquired Glenart business.
Volume spikes or a breakout above the recent 52‑week support level if the stock shows early signs of recovery.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a clean 2‑week volume spike combined with a price move above the last low. A positive earnings surprise will be the key confirmation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the very early phase of a potential mean‑reversion cycle – capitulation likely finished, but the bounce is still unconfirmed. A breakout or earnings beat would be the trigger.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If IGR recovers to 50% of its pre‑crash valuation – roughly a 10‑fold jump from its current price – the upside is more than 900%. The historical RED signal data show that deep crashes often deliver 160%+ returns, so a modest rebound could be very profitable.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Continued cash burn and failure to secure new contracts could push the stock lower. If earnings miss by a wide margin, the stock could fall another 30%‑40% before a recovery even starts.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped at the current price (essentially zero). Upside could be 10‑15× the entry price if the company hits the mid‑range of its 52‑week swing, giving a risk‑reward ratio of at least 1:10.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the recent low, size 2% of portfolio, stop 5% below that low. If the price breaks above the 52‑week support with volume > 150% of average, tighten the stop to 3% below the breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the June 16 earnings report. If the guidance is positive and volume spikes, consider a small entry. If the earnings miss, wait for a clear sign of a turnaround before re‑entering.
playbook.if_passing: No immediate terminal risk is evident. The only real risk would be a sudden liquidity crisis or regulatory issue, which is unlikely given the current data.

ai_insights.bottom_line: IGR is a deeply discounted logistics player with a clear restructuring plan; the June earnings release is the likely catalyst for a high‑reward mean‑reversion play, and the risk is limited to the current price.
